game_name: Bulls vs. Lakers and the NBA Playoffs

danger_signals.visual_form: contrasting team colors and player uniforms
danger_signals.meaning: visual identification of opposing threat in competitive play

danger_signals.visual_form: score display on screen showing point deficit
danger_signals.meaning: numerical indicator of losing position and game jeopardy

danger_signals.visual_form: foul counter visible in HUD or player status
danger_signals.meaning: risk of player elimination from game (foul-out condition)

safety_signals.visual_form: matching team colors and designated player uniforms

safety_signals.meaning: numerical indicator of winning position and game security

safety_signals.visual_form: ball control visualization or possession timer
safety_signals.meaning: player team has active control of play

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Lakers uniforms appear in opposing colors when Bulls are player faction
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: team bench, court positioning, crowd reaction cues

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Bulls uniforms appear in opposing colors when Lakers are player faction
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: team bench, court positioning, crowd reaction cues

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: standard court layout, normal crowd density, standard uniform display
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: standard player AI behavior, normal game pacing

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: playoff court markings, intensified crowd visualization, heightened uniform significance
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: increased opponent aggression, higher defensive pressure, more strategic play

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: clock display becomes prominent, score gap highlighted
large score differential prominently displayed, potential visual fatigue cues
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: reduced difficulty scaling, more predictable opponent patterns

environmental_storytelling.example: home/away court presentation differentiates Bulls vs Lakers venue through crowd presence and lighting
player statistics and names displayed communicate historical NBA matchup context (Jordan, Magic Johnson era)
real-time score, quarter progression, and time display create temporal narrative of game progression
crowd reaction intensity and audio cues reflect game state tension and momentum shifts

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: highlighted ball carrier, directional control enabled, shooting/passing options available
positioned between player and basket, active blocking animations, pursuit behavior
visual indicator (highlight or icon), movement toward open court positions
whistle audio cue, foul animation, official hand signals, counter increment

notes: Sports paradigm relies primarily on quantitative score/stat display combined with uniform color differentiation for faction identification. World-state communication is minimal beyond game mechanics (possession, score, time). Manifestation patterns emphasize: (1) team color coding as primary faction signal, (2) numerical HUD elements as danger/safety indicators, (3) real-time game clock as temporal state communicator. Political/narrative elements minimal in 1992 sports sim context—communication is mechanical rather than thematic.
